Participants gained valuable takeaways on discovering the leader within, building confidence, improving communication skills, and strengthening teamwork abilities. The webinar encouraged youth to take initiative, lead with purpose, and remain resilient in the pursuit of their goals.

The Rotaract Club of KSSEM actively supported the initiative by encouraging participation and contributing to the outreach of the event. Overall, Arise, Awake & Lead was a meaningful and impactful session that successfully blended inspiration, leadership, and learning, truly reflecting the ideals of Swami Vivekananda and the spirit of Rotaract service and collaboration.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Acropolis Royals, RID 3040
Avenues of Service: Community Service, Professional Development, Club Service

Name: Mental Health Matters
Description:
The Rotaract Club of Meraki (RID 3011) and the Rotaract Club of SVVV (RID 3040) jointly organized a meaningful awareness initiative titled “Mental Health Matters”, an online mental health awareness quiz aimed at promoting empathy, understanding, and open conversations around mental well-being. This event was organized by the Rotaract Club of Meraki and the Rotaract Club of SVVV, and the Rotaract Club of KSSEM collaborated for this wonderful initiative.

The quiz was conducted through Google Forms, with participation open until 10th January, allowing individuals to engage at their convenience. The initiative focused on breaking the stigma surrounding mental health and encouraging participants to acknowledge emotional well-being as an essential part of overall health. Through thoughtfully framed questions, the quiz highlighted the importance of self-care, awareness, and seeking support when needed.

“Mental Health Matters” created a safe and inclusive space for learning, reminding participants that mental health is a journey and that it is okay to talk and seek help. The digital format enabled wide participation across clubs, fostering collective responsibility toward mental health advocacy. 

Collaboration was encouraged with a minimum participation requirement of five members per club, and collaboration certificates were provided to participating clubs.

The Rotaract Club of KSSEM actively supported the initiative by encouraging participation and contributing to the spread of awareness among its members. The event successfully reinforced the message that no one is alone and that conversations around mental well-being are both necessary and empowering.

Overall, Mental Health Matters stood as a thoughtful and impactful initiative that reflected the Rotaract spirit of service, awareness, and unity while prioritizing mental well-being within the community.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Meraki (RID 3011) and the Rotaract Club of SVVV (RID 3040)
Avenues of Service: Community Service, Professional Development, Club Service

Name: Cyber Crime Laws in India
Description:
The Rotaract Club of Acropolis Professionals (RID 3040), in co-host with the Rotaract Club of YCCE (RID 3030), successfully organized an awareness session titled “Cyber Crime Laws in India” on 11th January 2026 via Google Meet. This event was organized by the Rotaract Club of Acropolis Professionals, and the Rotaract Club of KSSEM collaborated for this wonderful initiative.

The session aimed to educate participants on the growing threats in the digital space and the importance of understanding cyber laws in India. In an era where technology evolves rapidly, the session emphasized awareness as the first line of defense against cyber crimes. The event witnessed active participation from Rotaractor's across multiple clubs, reflecting the relevance and urgency of the topic.

The session was led by Rtr. Adv. Tejus Jain, Vice Chair L&D of Rotaract India MDIO, Legal Advisor of RID 3040, and an advocate with over five years of litigation experience. He provided valuable insights into cyber crime laws, covering topics such as online fraud, identity theft, digital rights, cyber harassment, and AI-generated deepfakes. Real-life examples and case references helped participants understand the legal consequences of cyber offences in a clear and practical manner.

Participants also gained guidance on recognizing cyber threats, protecting personal data, and responding responsibly to online fraud. The interactive nature of the session encouraged questions and discussions, making it informative and engaging.

The Rotaract Club of KSSEM actively supported the initiative by encouraging participation and contributing to its successful outreach. Overall, Cyber Crime Laws in India was a highly impactful session that strengthened digital awareness and empowered participants with essential legal knowledge for navigating the online world safely.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Acropolis Professionals (RID 3040), in co-host with the Rotaract Club of YCCE (RID 3030)
Avenues of Service: Community Service, Professional Development, Club Service

Name: Right To Learn
Description:
The Rotaract Club of GHRUA successfully conducted an awareness-based initiative titled “Right To Learn”, an engaging online quiz dedicated to celebrating the legacy of Savitribai Phule, India’s first woman teacher and a pioneering social reformer. This event was organized by the Rotaract Club of GHRUA, and the Rotaract Club of KSSEM collaborated for this wonderful initiative.

The quiz was launched on 4th January and concluded on 9th January, hosted on Google Forms, enabling participation from Rotaractors across different clubs. The initiative aimed to highlight Savitribai Phule’s contributions to education, equality, and social justice while spreading awareness about the importance of learning as a fundamental right. Carefully curated questions encouraged participants to reflect on her life, struggles, and vision for an inclusive and educated society.

The digital format made the initiative accessible and interactive, allowing participants to engage with history in a meaningful and informative way. Collaboration was encouraged through a minimum participation requirement of five members per club, fostering inter-club involvement and collective learning. Collaboration certificates were provided to participating clubs, strengthening fellowship among Rotaractors.

The Rotaract Club of KSSEM actively supported the initiative by promoting participation and encouraging members to engage in this knowledge-driven event. The quiz successfully combined education with awareness, reinforcing the relevance of Savitribai Phule’s ideals in today’s society.

Overall, Right To Learn stood as a thoughtful tribute to a visionary reformer and reflected the Rotaract spirit of learning, leadership, and service through impactful collaboration.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of GHRUA

Name: Unity in Action
Description:
The Rotaract Club of KSSEM proudly participated in the national initiative “Unity in Action”, organized by the Rotaract Club of Acropolis Professionals, RID 3040, in celebration of India’s 77th Republic Day. As part of this collaborative project, our club actively contributed under the Literacy Service Avenue, aligning with the project’s vision of education, unity, and national pride.

The initiative was conducted during the project window from 1st January to 26th January 2026, with the objective of educating young minds about India’s rich freedom struggle and constitutional values. Under the Literacy Service component, the Rotaract Club of KSSEM organized an engaging session focused on Indian freedom fighters and the significance of Republic Day, designed especially for students.

The session incorporated interactive methods such as storytelling, discussions, and visual learning to make history accessible and meaningful for children. By highlighting the sacrifices, courage, and values upheld by freedom fighters, the activity aimed to instill a sense of patriotism, responsibility, and respect for the Constitution among participants. The session encouraged students to express their thoughts, ask questions, and reflect on India’s journey as a sovereign democratic nation.

Members of the Rotaract Club of KSSEM actively planned and executed the activity, ensuring effective engagement and impactful learning outcomes. The submission of event documentation and reports was completed as per the guidelines of the organizing club.

Through this initiative, the Rotaract Club of KSSEM successfully contributed to nation-building through literacy, while strengthening inter-district collaboration and reaffirming its commitment to service, education, and unity. The project truly embodied the spirit of Unity in Action by transforming celebration into meaningful service.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Acropolis Professionals, RID 3040
Avenues of Service: International Service, Public Image, Professional Development, Public Relations

Name: Global Chapters ’25 – Phase 3
Description:
The Rotaract Club of KSSEM proudly participated in a cultural exchange initiative under Global Chapters ’25 – Phase 3: Threads of Culture, organized by the International Service Avenue of the Rotaract Club of University of Sri Jayewardenepura on 25 January 2026 at 8:00 PM IST.

The event brought together multiple Rotaract clubs from different regions, creating a vibrant platform for cultural interaction and global fellowship. A key highlight of the initiative was the letter exchange, through which participating clubs shared insights into their cultural heritage, traditions, values, and Rotaract experiences, fostering meaningful connections beyond geographical boundaries.

The exchange encouraged participants to appreciate diversity while recognizing the shared spirit of service and leadership. Members actively engaged in discussions, reflections, and cultural sharing, making the session interactive and enriching.

The Rotaract Club of KSSEM contributed enthusiastically to the initiative and was recognized for its valuable participation and collaboration. The event strengthened inter-club relationships and reinforced the importance of cultural understanding, communication, and international cooperation.

Overall, Threads of Culture successfully celebrated unity in diversity, weaving together cultures, stories, and friendships across borders, in alignment with Rotary’s vision of global connection and service.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of University of Sri Jayewardenepura
Avenues of Service: International Service, Public Image, Public Relations

Name: Connecting India
Description:
The Rotaract Club of KSSEM was pleased to collaborate with the Rotaract Club of Chandigarh and partner clubs for a cultural exchange initiative titled “Connecting India.” The event was conducted on 28 January 2026 at 8:30 PM IST in a virtual format.

The session aimed to celebrate India’s rich cultural diversity by bringing together participants from different regions of the country. Through multilingual expressions, cultural narratives, and shared perspectives, the initiative highlighted the unity that exists beyond geographical and linguistic boundaries.

Participants were encouraged to showcase elements of their regional culture, traditions, and values, creating an engaging platform for learning, appreciation, and mutual respect. The event fostered meaningful conversations and helped participants understand how cultural diversity strengthens national identity.

Members from various Rotaract clubs actively participated and contributed to the success of the event, making it interactive and inclusive. The exchange not only promoted cultural awareness but also strengthened inter-club bonding and collaboration.

Overall, “Connecting India” successfully reinforced the spirit of unity in diversity, aligning with Rotary’s vision of building connections beyond horizons while nurturing friendship, cultural pride, and national harmony.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Chandigarh
Avenues of Service: International Service, Public Image, Public Relations

Name: ANISUTIDE – The Club Hopping of DRRE
Description:
ANISUTIDE – The Club Hopping of DRRE was a meaningful club service interaction session conducted on 27th January 2026 at Forum South Bengaluru. As part of this district initiative, the Rotaract Club of KSSEM had the privilege of hosting DRRE Rtn. Rtr. Anirudh Kulkarni, offering members an opportunity to directly engage with district leadership and gain insights into district functioning and future initiatives.

The session began with introductions by the Board of Directors, who outlined their roles and responsibilities, followed by an address by the DRRE. He provided an overview of the district structure, upcoming plans, and key initiatives for the forthcoming Rotary year, emphasizing the importance of inter-club collaboration and active member engagement. He also shared his vision of creating a common digital platform to enhance communication and coordination among clubs.

Key district events, including the District Conference, and the “Dollar of Happiness” initiative were highlighted, encouraging members to participate actively. The interaction further allowed members to raise queries regarding sister club collaborations, government school permissions, social media coordination, and design support. The DRRE addressed these concerns with constructive suggestions, fostering an open and productive discussion.

The session was successfully executed through the coordinated efforts of the Core Committee and Board of Directors. Overall, ANISUTIDE strengthened the connection between the district leadership and the Rotaract Club of KSSEM, motivating members to actively contribute toward district initiatives and collaborative growth within the Rotaract fraternity.

Name: ANNASANTARPANE 3.0 – THREE YEARS OF COMPASSIONATE GIVING
Description:
ANNASANTARPANE 3.0 – THREE YEARS OF COMPASSIONATE GIVING

On the auspicious occasion of the Ram Mandir Inauguration, the Rotaract Club of KSSEM successfully organized Annasantarpane 3.0, a flagship community service initiative that beautifully blended devotion with compassionate action. Now in its third consecutive year, Annasantarpane stands as a testament to the club’s unwavering commitment to serving humanity through the noble act of food donation.

This year’s edition was made possible through the generous support of around 30 individual sponsors and one collaborating club, with a total amount of ₹17,500 collected. The project proudly collaborated with the Rotaract Club of JP Nagar, whose support added strength and unity to this meaningful initiative. In addition, 35 individuals contributed personally, reaffirming that even small acts of kindness can create a significant impact.

As part of the initiative, nutritious Tomato Bath was carefully prepared, packed, and distributed to 500 people from families in need. Of these, 400 food packets were distributed along Holiday Village Road, Mallasandra, while the remaining 100 packets were served near the Banashankari Temple. Every step from preparation to distribution was carried out with dignity, discipline, and a deep sense of responsibility.

Annasantarpane 3.0 was not merely a food distribution drive; it was a heartfelt offering of love, hope, and respect. Each meal served symbolized the spirit of Annadaan, reminding everyone involved that true devotion finds its highest expression in selfless service.

With this third successful edition, Annasantarpane has rightfully earned its place as a Flagship Project of the Rotaract Club of KSSEM. The smiles received, the hunger eased, and the unity displayed reaffirmed the club’s belief that service, when driven by faith and compassion, has the power to transform lives.
Venue: Holiday Village Road, Mallasandra & Banashankari Temple
Areas of Focus: Growing Local Economies, Providing Clean Water, Sanitation and Hygiene
Avenues of Service: Public Image, Community Service, Public Relations

Name: District Learning Program (DLP) of RID 3080
Description:
The Rotaract Club of KSSEM had the privilege of being represented at a district-level engagement with Rotaract District 3080 during a visit to Chandigarh. Representing the club were Rtr. Mahesh N, International Service Director, and Rtr. Thanushree N, Resource Manager.

The visit coincided with the District Learning Program (DLP) of RID 3080, providing an excellent opportunity for the club representatives to interact with the district leadership team, including the District Rotaract Representative Rtr. Ritik Nagpal and other district officials.

During the program, the representatives had the opportunity to engage with members of various district avenues, understand ongoing projects, and observe best practices followed by the district. The interaction also facilitated meaningful discussions, inter-district networking, and exchange of ideas, strengthening bonds between the two districts.

This exposure proved to be a highly enriching experience, enhancing the club’s understanding of structured leadership, collaboration, and large-scale Rotaract operations. The visit reaffirmed the Rotaract Club of KSSEM’s commitment to learning, growth, and building strong connections beyond district boundaries, contributing positively to the club’s overall development and international service outlook.
Host Club: District Learning Program (DLP) of RID 3080

Name: Investment Insights
Description:
The “Investment Insights” online learning session, organized by the Rotaract Club of Bengaluru United, RID:3191, in collaboration with The Rotaract Club of KSSEM, RID:3191, was successfully conducted on 21st December 2025 at 7:00 PM IST via Google Meet.

The session was designed to simplify the world of finance and equip participants with essential knowledge on investment basics, risk management, diversification and current market trends, enabling them to make informed and confident financial decisions.

The event featured Rtr. Shubhakruth, an MCom graduate and active investment and securities trader, as the resource person. He delivered an engaging and insightful session, breaking down complex financial concepts into simple, relatable explanations. His analytical approach, calm communication style and practical examples helped participants gain clarity and confidence in understanding investments and market behavior.

The session witnessed active participation from members across various Rotaract clubs, fulfilling the collaboration requirements and encouraging meaningful interaction and learning. Participants appreciated the speaker’s balanced perspective and practical insights, especially on managing risks and building a disciplined investment mindset.

The event concluded on a successful and positive note, with appreciation extended to the organizing team from both clubs for their effective coordination and execution. The initiative successfully promoted financial literacy, professional development, and inter-club collaboration, making Investment Insights a valuable learning experience for all attendees.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Bengaluru United, RID:3191

Name: ISD Meet
Description:
The District International Service Team, RID 3191, organized Royal Horizons 2.0, a district-level meet-up for International Service Directors (ISDs) of various Rotaract clubs, on 14th December 2025 at 12:30 PM at SSMRV Degree College, Jayanagar. Our Club ISD, Rtr. Mahesh N, participated in the meeting along with ISDs from different clubs across the district.

The session provided an open platform for all ISDs to come together and share updates regarding their respective club activities. During the interaction, each club, including ours, presented details about the international service events conducted so far, the experiences gained, and the plans for upcoming initiatives. This exchange helped in understanding the diverse approaches followed by clubs and encouraged idea sharing.

The meeting also allowed ISDs to raise queries and concerns related to planning, collaboration, and reporting of international service projects. These queries were addressed by the district team through clarifications and guidance, ensuring better clarity and alignment for future activities.

Overall, the meet-up created a supportive environment for discussion, learning, and coordination among ISDs. It strengthened inter-club communication and helped in building a clear roadmap for effective international service execution across the district.
Venue: SSMRV College, Jayanagar 4th T Block, Bengaluru
Host Club: District 3191

Name: Touch the Sky with Glory
Description:
The Rotaract Club of KSSEM was delighted to collaborate with the Rotaract Club of Pune Mideast for an initiative titled “Touch the Sky with Glory.” The event was conducted on 27 December 2025 at 11:00 AM IST.

The session aimed to provide participants with valuable insights into the world of military aviation, focusing on the journey of becoming a pilot officer in the Indian Armed Forces. It highlighted the importance of discipline, mindset, and strategic preparation required to clear competitive selection processes such as the SSB interview.

The event was conducted virtually via Google Meet and featured Rtr. Prateek Hanchate, who has successfully cleared the SSB Interview and CPSS for the Indian Navy Pilot. The speaker shared his personal journey, preparation techniques, challenges faced, and practical guidance, making the session informative and inspiring for aspiring candidates.

Members of the Rotaract Club of KSSEM actively participated in the session and benefited from the interactive discussion and real-life experiences shared. The initiative motivated participants to explore career opportunities in defense services with clarity and confidence.

This collaboration strengthened inter-club bonding and reinforced our commitment to career guidance and professional development. The session successfully inspired young minds to pursue excellence, discipline, and service to the nation.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Pune Mideast
Avenues of Service: International Service, Community Service, Professional Development, Club Service

Name: Party in Pink 4.0
Description:
The Rotaract Club of KSSEM actively participated in Party in Pink 4.0, a vibrant Zumba-based awareness and fundraising initiative organized by the Rotaract Club of Bangalore JP Nagar on 14th December 2025 at SSMRV College, Jayanagar. The event was conducted with the objective of creating awareness about breast cancer and raising funds to support women battling the disease.

Members of the Rotaract Club of KSSEM enthusiastically joined the programme and took part in the Zumba session, contributing to the energetic and supportive atmosphere of the event. Through active participation, our club stood in solidarity with the cause, promoting the importance of early detection, awareness, and community support in the collective fight against breast cancer.

The event witnessed participation from Rotaractors, students, and members of the public, all coming together in shades of pink to symbolize hope, strength, and resilience. Along with physical fitness and fun, the programme successfully delivered a powerful message on women’s health and the need for continuous awareness and advocacy.

By participating in Party in Pink 4.0, the Rotaract Club of KSSEM reaffirmed its commitment to community service, health awareness, and collaborative initiatives within the Rotaract fraternity. The event served as a meaningful platform to contribute toward a social cause while fostering unity and shared responsibility among clubs.
Venue: SSMRV College, Jayanagar 4th T Block, Bengaluru
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Bangalore JP Nagar
Avenues of Service: International Service, Public Image, Community Service

Name: Plates of Hope 🍛
Description:
As part of its ongoing commitment to community welfare and social responsibility, the Rotaract Club of KSSEM successfully conducted a Food Distribution Drive on 20th December 2025 in Uttarahalli, Bengaluru. The initiative aimed to support underprivileged and needy individuals by providing them with freshly prepared, nutritious meals and spreading warmth and compassion during the winter season.

The drive was organized with the intention of addressing food insecurity among daily wage workers, homeless individuals, and economically disadvantaged families in the area. Members of the club actively participated in the planning and execution of the programme, ensuring that the distribution was carried out in a respectful, organized, and dignified manner.

On the day of the event, Rotaractors gathered at the distribution site and efficiently coordinated the process of serving food to the beneficiaries. Care was taken to maintain hygiene standards while distributing the meals. The food packets were prepared to be wholesome and filling, keeping in mind the nutritional needs of the recipients. Each beneficiary was served with empathy and kindness, reinforcing the spirit of service that Rotaract stands for.

The response from the beneficiaries was heartwarming. Many expressed their gratitude and appreciation for the timely support, as the meals provided relief and comfort to those struggling to meet their daily needs. The smiles on their faces served as a powerful reminder of the positive impact that collective effort and compassion can create in the community.

This food distribution drive was not just an act of charity but a meaningful step toward building a more caring and inclusive society. It strengthened the bond between the club and the local community while giving members an opportunity to actively engage in hands-on service. Through this initiative, the Rotaract Club of KSSEM reaffirmed its commitment to serving humanity, addressing social challenges, and making a difference through sustained community service efforts.

The club looks forward to organizing more such initiatives in the future, continuing its mission to uplift and support those in need.
Areas of Focus: Growing Local Economies, Providing Clean Water, Sanitation and Hygiene

Name: Volunteering Service – Turahalli Government School Annual Day
Description:
The Rotaract Club of KSSEM actively volunteered for the Annual Day celebration of Turahalli Government School on 13th December 2025. This meaningful community service initiative was undertaken with the objective of supporting the school in successfully organizing their annual celebration and ensuring smooth execution of the entire program.

A total of seven Rotaractors from our club contributed both directly at the venue and through backend coordination. Our members were involved in stage arrangement, student coordination, program scheduling support, seating arrangements, and overall event management. Backend volunteers handled communication, material arrangements, and coordination with school authorities to ensure that all requirements were met on time.

The team worked closely with the school staff and Parent Rotary members to maintain proper flow of the program and provide necessary assistance throughout the event. Their dedication and teamwork played a vital role in making the Annual Day celebration organized, disciplined, and memorable for the students, teachers, and parents.

This volunteering activity reflected the club’s commitment towards community development, educational support, and responsible citizenship. It also strengthened the bond between our club and the school while giving our members an opportunity to serve society through active participation.
Areas of Focus: Supporting Education, Saving Mothers and Children
Avenues of Service: Public Image, Community Service, Public Relations

Name: Pulse polio
Description:
On the occasion of Polio National Immunisation Day (NID) 2025), we, the Rotaract Club of KSSEM, actively took part in the immunisation drive by volunteering at the inaugural ceremony held at the Primary Health Centre, Uttarahalli, Bengaluru. The initiative reflected our Club’s commitment to community health, service, and social responsibility.

The inaugural programme was graced by the presence of President Rtn. Srinivas Naidu R, Secretary Rtn. Karthikraj G, PP Dr. K. S. Naidu, PP Rtn. Sandeep V. Nijagal, along with enthusiastic Rotaractors from KSSEM, Yeshwanth, and other members. Several local dignitaries, including Mr. Ramesh Raju, Ex-Corporator, were also present and extended their support to the cause.

As part of our volunteering efforts, members of the Rotaract Club of KSSEM actively assisted health workers and volunteers involved in the immunisation process. We also supported the smooth conduct of the programme by helping coordinate breakfast and lunch for nearly 120 volunteers, who worked tirelessly to ensure the successful implementation of the immunisation drive.

Our participation in Polio NID 2025 highlighted the importance of collective action in safeguarding children’s health and strengthening public health initiatives. Through this volunteering effort, the Rotaract Club of KSSEM reaffirmed its dedication to preventive healthcare, community engagement, and meaningful service.
Areas of Focus: Providing Clean Water, Sanitation and Hygiene, Promoting Peace, Fighting Disease

Name: Art of Public Speaking – Speak Like a Leader
Description:
The Rotaract Club of Acropolis Professionals RID 3040, in co host with the Rotaract Club of Karpagam Academy of Higher Education RID 3206, successfully organized an impactful leadership session titled “Art of Public Speaking – Speak Like a Leader” on 29th December 2025 at 8:00 PM IST via Google Meet. The session was designed to enhance communication skills and build confidence among participants, empowering them to express ideas with clarity and leadership presence.

The session was led by Rtr. Yatharth Golchha, Chairman of Rotaract India and MDIO Chief Advisor of RI District 3040. With his vast leadership experience, he delivered an engaging and insightful session that focused on the core elements of effective public speaking. He spoke about overcoming stage fear, structuring impactful speeches, and understanding the importance of preparation and practice. The speaker emphasized how voice modulation, body language, and storytelling play a crucial role in influencing audiences and communicating with confidence.

Rtr. Yatharth Golchha shared practical techniques and real life examples, making the session highly relatable and easy to understand. Participants gained valuable insights into how strong communication skills contribute to leadership effectiveness in both personal and professional spaces. The interactive nature of the session kept the audience engaged and motivated throughout.

The event was conducted as a collaborative initiative, with a minimum requirement of five members per participating club. The session received an enthusiastic response from Rotaractors across districts, reflecting its relevance and importance.

Overall, Art of Public Speaking – Speak Like a Leader was a highly enriching experience that inspired participants to speak with confidence, connect with audiences, and lead with impact through effective communication.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Acropolis Professionals RID 3040

Name: Body & Being – Conversations That Heal
Description:
The Rotaract Club of Delhi Genesis, nurtured by the Rotary Club of Delhi Genesis, successfully conducted an insightful online session titled “Body & Being – Conversations That Heal (Phase 1)” on 23rd December 2025 at 7:00 PM via Google Meet. The session focused on promoting mental well-being, self-awareness, and holistic personal growth through meaningful dialogue and reflection.

The session was led by Dr. Sneha Vashisht, Founder of Hapodition, an Organisational Behaviour Psychotherapist and Master Trainer with expertise in leadership development, holistic wellness, and soft skills. She delivered a deeply engaging and thoughtful session that emphasized the strong connection between the body and the mind. Through her calm and empathetic approach, she encouraged participants to acknowledge emotions, understand internal patterns, and begin healing through conscious conversations.

Dr. Sneha spoke about the importance of emotional intelligence, self-compassion, and mindful awareness in both personal and professional life. She highlighted how unresolved emotions can affect overall well-being and how simple reflective practices can help individuals regain balance and clarity. The session created a safe and open space for participants to reflect, listen, and learn without judgment.

The event was conducted as a collaborative initiative, with a minimum requirement of ten members per club, and collaboration certificates were offered to participating clubs. The session was well received and appreciated for its relevance, warmth, and depth.
Overall, Body & Being – Conversations That Heal (Phase 1) was a meaningful initiative that reinforced the importance of mental wellness, self-care, and empathetic conversations, leaving participants with valuable insights and a sense of inner calm.
Host Club: Rotary Club of Delhi Genesis
Avenues of Service: Community Service, Professional Development, Club Service

Name: Breaking the Silence Mental Health at Work
Description:
The Rotaract Club of Mount Carmel College organized an impactful online session titled “Breaking the Silence: Mental Health at Work” on 23rd December, conducted via Google Meet from 6:00 PM to 7:00 PM. The session aimed to create awareness about mental health in professional environments and encourage open conversations around emotional well-being.

The session was led by Mr. Anmol Sanghai, who delivered an insightful and thoughtful discussion on the importance of mental health in the workplace. He addressed common challenges such as stress, burnout, emotional pressure, and the stigma often associated with mental health concerns. Through clear explanations and relatable examples, the speaker emphasized the need for emotional resilience, self-care, and supportive work cultures.

Participants gained a better understanding of how mental health directly impacts productivity, relationships, and overall job satisfaction. The session highlighted practical ways to recognize mental health concerns, manage stress effectively, and maintain a healthy work-life balance. The speaker also encouraged participants to seek help when needed and to support others by fostering empathy and understanding in professional spaces.

The event was conducted as a collaborative initiative, with a requirement of five members per participating club. E-certificates were provided to all registered participants. The session was well-received and appreciated for its relevance, clarity, and meaningful message.

Overall, Breaking the Silence served as an important step toward promoting mental health awareness and reinforcing the value of emotional well-being in today’s fast-paced work environment.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Mount Carmel College
Avenues of Service: Community Service, Professional Development, Club Service

Name: From Potential to Performance
Description:
The Rotaract Club of Mount Carmel College successfully organized a personal development session titled “From Potential to Performance” on 27th December, conducted virtually via Google Meet from 6:15 PM to 7:30 PM. The session was designed to help participants understand how to recognize their inner potential and effectively convert it into consistent personal and professional performance.

The speaker for the session was Ms. Sonali Punj Singh, who delivered an engaging and insightful talk focused on mindset development, self-awareness, and confidence building. She explained the importance of understanding one’s strengths and limitations and emphasized how clarity of purpose plays a key role in achieving sustained growth. Through simple examples and practical strategies, she guided participants on improving productivity, building resilience, and maintaining confidence in challenging situations.

The session highlighted key takeaways such as self-awareness for personal growth, performance-enhancing strategies, and the development of confidence and emotional resilience. Participants found the discussion relatable and useful, as the concepts were easy to understand and applicable to both academic and professional life.

The event was conducted as a collaborative initiative, with a minimum participation requirement of four members per club. E-certificates were provided to all registered participants. Overall, the session was informative, motivating, and impactful, helping attendees gain valuable insights into transforming potential into measurable performance and encouraging continuous self-improvement.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Mount Carmel College

Name: Belaku
Description:
ಬೆಳಕು was a heartfelt community service initiative carried out in collaboration with Rotary District 3191, symbolizing hope, care, and sustainable living. Through this project, our club took a meaningful step toward supporting underprivileged families by distributing 18 solar lamps to needy households in nearby slum areas.

The initiative aimed not only to provide light but also to bring safety, comfort, and dignity into the everyday lives of the beneficiaries. Each solar lamp distributed under ಬೆಳಕು operates using the power of sunlight. During the daytime, the lamps charge naturally, and at night they provide bright, reliable illumination without the need for electricity. This made them especially valuable for families with limited or no access to a stable power supply. The lamps are eco-friendly, cost-effective, and easy to use, making them a sustainable solution for daily living.

Along with the distribution, our team, along with the support of District 3191, patiently explained how to use and maintain the solar lamps to every family. The beneficiaries were guided on proper charging, usage hours, and basic care, ensuring that the lamps could be used effectively for a long period. Special attention was given to elderly members and children so that everyone could confidently operate the lamps.

The smiles and gratitude of the 20 families clearly reflected the impact of this initiative. For many homes, these lamps meant safer nights, better visibility for children to study, and relief from darkness.

ಬೆಳಕು was more than just a solar lamp distribution drive; it was a reminder that small actions, when supported through meaningful collaboration, can create lasting change. By lighting homes, we also lit hearts—reinforcing our commitment to service, sustainability, and social responsibility.

Name: Science of Meditation – Where Science Meets Inner Calm
Description:
The Rotaract Club of KSSEM, RID 3191, successfully conducted an enriching online session titled “Science of Meditation – Where Science Meets Inner Calm” on 30th December 2025 via Zoom Workspace. The session witnessed remarkable global participation, with 41 collaborating Rotaract clubs and over 100 participants joining from different parts of the world, making it a truly impactful international initiative.

The session was led by Ms. Divya Buddha, a Meditation Coach and an inspiring practitioner who began her meditation journey at the young age of eight. She shared her personal experiences and spoke about how meditation introduced natural happiness, inner joy, and mental clarity into her life from an early stage. Her journey resonated deeply with the audience, especially the youth, as she highlighted how consistent meditation practice can bring balance and peace even in demanding environments.

During the session, Ms. Divya Buddha explained the benefits of meditation, including improved concentration, emotional stability, enhanced memory, and overall mental well-being. A key highlight of the session was her powerful message on how meditation can transform youth from blunders to wonders, helping individuals achieve clarity, discipline, and self-awareness. To provide a practical experience, she guided the participants through a live Anapanasati meditation, allowing everyone to experience mindfulness and calmness firsthand.

The session was interactive, insightful, and deeply calming, leaving participants with a sense of inner peace and renewed perspective. Overall, the event was a grand success, reflecting the club’s commitment to promoting mental well-being, mindfulness, and holistic growth through meaningful global collaborations.

Name: Eye Checkup Camp
Description:
The Eye Checkup Camp was successfully organized at KSIT by the Rotaract Club of KSSEM in partnership with Rotary Bangalore Vidyapeeth on 22 November. The primary objective of the camp was to promote eye health awareness and provide free, accessible eye screening services to students, faculty, and staff.

The camp began with the presence of Rotarians, faculty coordinators, medical professionals, and Rotaract volunteers who ensured efficient arrangements and smooth flow of activities. A team of certified optometrists conducted comprehensive examinations including vision testing, refractive error detection, and basic consultation. Participants also received guidance on eye care practices, preventive measures, and the importance of regular checkups.

The event witnessed an excellent response, with beneficiaries availing the screening services. Several participants were advised on corrective lenses or required follow-up consultations, helping identify potential vision concerns at an early stage.

The members of the Rotaract Club of KSSEM actively contributed to registration, crowd management, coordination with doctors, and assisting participants. Their teamwork and dedication played a vital role in the smooth execution of the camp, reflecting the club’s commitment to community welfare and health initiatives.

The Eye Checkup Camp concluded successfully, creating awareness on the importance of eye health while providing valuable medical support to the KSIT community. The collaboration with Rotary strengthened the impact of the initiative and highlighted the collective spirit of service.
Areas of Focus: Providing Clean Water, Sanitation and Hygiene

Name: Academic Excellence Recognition Programme
Description:
Academic Excellence Recognition Programme

The Academic Excellence Recognition Programme was successfully conducted by Rotary Bangalore Vidyapeeth, with the active support and participation of the Rotaract Club of KSSEM. The primary objective of the programme was to recognize and encourage government school students who have excelled in their academic performance and to motivate them to continue their journey toward excellence.

The programme commenced with a welcoming prayer song and dance, which created a positive and inspiring atmosphere. This was followed by addresses from the Chief Guest and other dignitaries, who emphasized the importance of education, discipline, and perseverance in shaping a bright future. Their words served as a source of inspiration for the students, teachers, parents, and volunteers present at the event.

One of the key highlights of the programme was the prize distribution ceremony, where deserving students were honored for their outstanding academic achievements. The students were felicitated in the presence of their parents, making the occasion both meaningful and memorable. The recognition not only celebrated academic excellence but also acknowledged the constant support and guidance provided by teachers and families.

Members of the Rotaract Club of KSSEM actively supported the event through coordination, volunteering, and engagement with students and parents, contributing to the smooth execution of the programme. It reflected a strong commitment to community service and youth development.

The programme concluded on a positive note, leaving the students motivated and encouraged to pursue their goals with confidence and determination. Overall, the event was impactful and well-organized, successfully reinforcing the value of education and the spirit of excellence.

Name: Kannada Rajyotsava 2025
Description:
Kannada Rajyotsava 2025 at KSSEM was a vibrant and heartfelt celebration that beautifully showcased the cultural spirit, unity, and pride of Karnataka. The event began on an auspicious note with an Invocation, followed by the soulful Naada Geethe, setting a serene and patriotic tone for the morning. The dignitaries were then warmly welcomed, marking the official commencement of the celebration .

The Flag Hoisting ceremony stood as a powerful moment of honour and respect, filling the atmosphere with deep cultural pride. The President, Secretary, and Treasurer each delivered short addresses, reflecting the significance of the occasion. This was followed by thoughtful and inspiring messages from the incharge Principal of KSSEM and principal of KSP, adding depth to the event’s purpose.

The cultural segment of the celebration brought the stage alive. The Singing Club delivered a melodious performance, leading into a captivating 30-minute presentation by the Veeraghase and Pooje Kunitha Team, which showcased Karnataka’s traditional folk vibrancy. Energetic contributions from the Dance Club, Theatre Club, KSP Faculty, First Year students, and the Fashion Club added colour, creativity, and enthusiasm to the day’s flow.

The event concluded with the National Anthem, followed by the dynamic beats of Tamate, creating an electrifying finale. Refreshments were served before dispersal, ending the celebration on a warm and joyful note.

Kannada Rajyotsava 2025 at KSSEM was a graceful blend of culture, tradition, and togetherness, honouring the rich heritage of Karnataka with pride and elegance.
Avenues of Service: Public Image, Community Service, Club Service, Public Relations

Name: ನಮ್ಮ ಕನ್ನಡ ನಮ್ಮ ಹೆಮ್ಮೆ
Description:
ನಮ್ಮ ಕನ್ನಡ ನಮ್ಮ ಹೆಮ್ಮೆ

The Rotaract Club of KSSEM proudly celebrated Kannada Rajyotsava through a heartfelt community service event titled ನಮ್ಮ ಕನ್ನಡ ನಮ್ಮ ಹೆಮ್ಮೆ. This initiative was created to honour the spirit of Kannada and to share the warmth of our culture with the people who work quietly every day to keep our city running. Our team visited the metro staff and the BMTC workers at Banashankari TTMC with the intention of expressing gratitude and spreading joy.

Carrying Karnataka flag badges, sweets and greeting cards, we approached each worker with respect and appreciation. These small yet meaningful offerings created beautiful moments of connection. The smiles we received and the conversations we shared reflected the pride and affection that Kannada evokes in all of us. It reminded us that celebration becomes complete only when it reaches the hearts of the community.

The event ನಮ್ಮ ಕನ್ನಡ ನಮ್ಮ ಹೆಮ್ಮೆ stood as a graceful expression of cultural pride and human kindness. It reflected the belief that Kannada is not just a language but an emotion that binds people together. By acknowledging the efforts of metro and BMTC staff, we honoured their daily contribution which often remains unnoticed yet plays an important role in the rhythm of our city.

As the colours of the Kannada flag brightened the atmosphere and the greetings filled the air, the event became a beautiful memory for everyone involved. It was a simple initiative that carried a powerful message of unity, respect and heartfelt pride for our land and language.
Areas of Focus: Growing Local Economies, Protecting The Environment
Avenues of Service: Public Image, Community Service, Public Relations

Name: Nada Habba
Description:
ನಾಡ ಹಬ್ಬ - Rotaract Kannada Rajyotsava was a vibrant and meaningful celebration held at Janapada Loka, Ramanagara, bringing Rotaractors together in honour of Karnataka’s rich cultural heritage. The day began with members gathering at Pattanagere Metro Station and traveling to the venue, building a sense of unity and shared excitement for the festivities ahead .

The event commenced with an energetic procession, followed by the dignified flag hoisting ceremony, setting a patriotic tone for the celebration. The MC’s opening remarks and the formal calling of the meeting to order were followed by the lighting of the lamp, symbolising positivity and the beginning of a culturally immersive experience.

An expressive inaugural dance beautifully captured the spirit of Karnataka. The President then delivered a warm welcome address, after which the DRR and District Youth Service Director shared their remarks, adding meaning and encouragement to the occasion. Their presence enriched the cultural significance of ನಾಡ ಹಬ್ಬ without extending beyond the planned agenda.

The introduction and address by the Chief Guest added value to the event, followed by the felicitation of the Guest of Honour, acknowledging their contribution. The stage then came alive with a graceful dance by the Rotaract Club of Shantiniketan, leading into a vibrant series of cultural dance performances that showcased the beauty of Karnataka’s traditions.

The celebration concluded with sponsor recognitions, a heartfelt vote of thanks, the national anthem, and a joyful lunch accompanied by music, creating a memorable and culturally rich ending to ನಾಡ ಹಬ್ಬ .
Host Club: RI District 3191

Name: Nodii Nama Uru 2.0
Description:
Nodi Namma Ooru 2.0 was a special cultural and fellowship initiative where members of Rotaract District 3141 visited Bengaluru and were warmly hosted by our club. The purpose of this experience was to give our guests a meaningful glimpse into the heritage, culture, and vibrant spirit of our city. Throughout their visit, our club ensured a smooth and welcoming hospitality experience, making the journey memorable for every participant.

The tour began with visits to iconic historical landmarks such as Bangalore Fort and Tipu Sultan’s Summer Palace, where the guests explored the city’s rich past and architectural elegance. They were then taken to the lively surroundings of KR Market, one of Bengaluru’s oldest and most colourful marketplaces, offering a taste of local life. The group also visited the majestic Vidhana Soudha, a symbol of Karnataka’s governance and grandeur.

To showcase Bengaluru’s unique blend of spirituality and serenity, the guests were guided to ISKCON Vaikunta Hill, where the peaceful atmosphere and scenic views added a refreshing touch to the day. Later, the energetic ambience of Church Street offered a modern and vibrant city experience.

As a part of celebrating Karnataka’s culture, our club proudly involved the guests in Rotary Kannada Rajyotsava and Namma Nada Habba – Rotaract Kannada Rajyotsava, allowing them to witness traditional performances, local flavours, and the cultural pride of the state.

Overall, Nodi Namma Ooru 2.0 was a beautifully curated blend of heritage, fellowship, and cultural exchange, strengthening bonds between districts and creating unforgettable memories for everyone involved.
Host Club: R

Name: Crack the Code: Resume to Interview
Description:
The Rotaract Club of Banashankari ,RID 3191 successfully hosted the online professional development session, “Crack the Code: Resume to Interview,” on 9th November 2025, at 6:00 PM on Google Meet. The event featured Ms. Sandhya Satyan, Former Vice President of Operations – India at Goldman Sachs and currently Head of People Transactions at Novotech, as the esteemed speaker. The session focused on career development, resume building, and interview skills. The event was well-attended and played a significant role in empowering participants with practical, career-transforming knowledge.

We were delighted by the strong support from the Rotaract Club of KSSEM, which actively participated in our event, sending 10 members from their Board of Directors (BODs) and successfully fulfilling the collaboration criteria.

Under the leadership of Rtr. Prajwal D(Professional Service Director) and Rtr. Sarath S (Professional Service Co Director),Rtr. Sahana BM (President,Rac Banashankari) the event concluded as a significant success, empowering attendees with practical, actionable strategies for career.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Banashankari ,RID 3191

Name: Your Network is Your Net-worth
Description:
The Rotaract Club of JIMS Vasant Kunj organized an online professional development session under Project Elevate titled “Your Network is Your Net-worth: The Power of Professional Networking.” The event was held on 22nd November 2025 at 7:00 PM on Google Meet. The purpose of the session was to help participants understand how building the right connections can support their future growth and open new opportunities.

The session featured Mr. Aditya Raj as the guest speaker. He is a TEDx Speaker, an MBA graduate from the Great Lakes Institute of Management, and currently works as the Community Manager at Great Learning. He also hosts The MBA Playbook, a YouTube series that offers practical guidance for MBA aspirants. During the session, he spoke about the importance of networking in today’s professional world. He explained how authentic relationships can lead to personal development, better career chances, and long-term success. He also shared simple methods to build and maintain meaningful connections.

The event was conducted in collaboration with multiple Rotaract clubs, with each club required to have a minimum of five participants. Collaboration certificates were provided to the clubs that participated. Overall, the session was informative and helped members understand the real value of professional networking.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of JIMS Vasant Kunj

Name: Silent Scars Impact of Sexual Abuse on Mental Health
Description:
The webinar titled “Silent Scars: Impact of Sexual Abuse on Mental Health” was thoughtfully organized by the Rotaract Club of Young Visionaries on 15th November 2025 at 7:00 PM via Google Meet. The session aimed to spread awareness, empathy, and understanding about the long-term psychological effects of sexual abuse, while creating a safe and sensitive space for learning.

The resource person for the event, Ms. Malika Narang, a Mental Health Advocate and Psychologist, shared deep insights into the emotional and psychological aftermath that survivors often experience, including anxiety, fear, guilt, and trust issues. She emphasized the importance of acknowledging survivors’ experiences, validating their feelings, and avoiding victim-blaming. The session also highlighted healthy coping mechanisms, the role of therapy, and the importance of timely psychological support in the healing journey.

Participants were guided on how to respond sensitively to survivors, listen without judgment, and encourage professional help when needed. The session stressed building a more compassionate and informed society where survivors feel seen, heard, and supported.

The event saw active participation from various clubs that met the collaboration criteria of a minimum of five members. Under the leadership of Rtr. Priyanka Bose (President), Rtr. Mehak Sharma (Vocational Services Director), and Rtr. Muskan (Event Chair), the webinar proved to be impactful, informative, and emotionally resonant.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Young Visionaries
Avenues of Service: Community Service, Professional Development, Club Service

Name: Breast Cancer Awareness Talk
Description:
The Breast Cancer Awareness Webinar, organized by the Rotaract Club of Delhi Rajdhani, RID 3011, was held on 16th November 2025 at 7:00 PM via Google Meet. This impactful and educational session aimed to promote early detection, dispel common myths, and encourage open conversations around breast cancer — a crucial health concern affecting women worldwide.

The event featured Dr. Avneet Kaur, a respected medical professional and health educator, as the guest speaker. She offered clear and compassionate insights into breast cancer, explaining its causes, symptoms, and risk factors. Dr. Avneet emphasized the life-saving importance of early detection, regular screenings, and monthly self-breast examinations. She also addressed common misconceptions surrounding the disease and shared motivating stories of courage and survival that inspired participants to prioritize their health.

The webinar included an interactive Q&A segment, allowing attendees to seek clarity on doubts and concerns. The participation from various Rotaract clubs met the collaboration criteria, ensuring strong engagement and collective learning across districts.

Spearheaded by Rtr. Yash Raj (Community Service Director), Rtr. Sudarshan Pandish (Secretary), and Rtr. Radhika Agarwal (President), the session concluded as a meaningful initiative that promoted awareness, emotional support, and the message that early action saves lives. This event successfully strengthened the importance of health education and community awareness within the Rotaract network.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Delhi Rajdhani, RID 3011

Name: Healthy Lifestyles Building Better Habits for a Better You
Description:
The “Healthy Lifestyles: Building Better Habits for a Better You” online session, jointly organized by the Rotaract Club of Dyal Singh College, RID 3012 and the Rotaract Club of Coimbatore Texcity, RID 3602, was successfully conducted on 8th November 2025 at 8:00 PM IST via Google Meet. The session focused on promoting holistic wellness by encouraging participants to adopt healthier habits in their daily lives.

The event featured Dt. Vidhee Shah as the esteemed speaker, who shared valuable insights on the importance of maintaining physical fitness, nurturing mental well-being, and following balanced nutritional practices. She highlighted how small lifestyle changes — such as mindful eating, staying active, proper hydration, and stress management — can significantly enhance overall health. Her practical guidance made the session relatable and easy to apply in everyday routines.

The event saw active participation from several Rotaract clubs, each meeting the collaboration requirement of a minimum of five members. Participants appreciated the speaker’s clarity, practical tips, and the session’s focus on sustainable long-term wellness rather than temporary fixes.

Under the leadership of Rtr. Vaishali Verma (International Service Director, RaCDSC), Rtr. N V Aruna Devi (President, RaCDSC), Rtr. Riti Achammal (International Service Director, RaC Coimbatore Texcity), and Rtr. Arjun (President, RaC Coimbatore Texcity), the event concluded as a meaningful and informative initiative dedicated to healthier living and collective well-being.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Dyal Singh College, RID 3012 and the Rotaract Club of Coimbatore Texcity, RID 3602

Name: Ayurveda Roots to Wellness
Description:
The “Ayurveda: Roots to Wellness” online session, organized by the Rotaract Club of AIMS, RID 3192, was successfully conducted on 7th November 2025 from 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM IST via Google Meet. The session aimed to introduce participants to the profound wisdom of Ayurveda and highlight its relevance in promoting holistic health in modern times.

The event featured Dr. Niveditha (BAMS), who delivered an enlightening talk on the fundamental principles of Ayurveda, its focus on balancing the mind, body, and spirit, and how simple lifestyle practices can improve long-term well-being. She also explained concepts such as doshas, natural healing, diet recommendations, and daily routines that help maintain harmony in everyday life.

The session witnessed enthusiastic participation from various Rotaract clubs, fulfilling the collaboration requirement of a minimum of eight members per club. Participants appreciated the practical insights offered by the speaker, especially regarding stress relief, immunity-boosting habits, and natural wellness approaches.

The event concluded on a positive note with appreciation for the organizing team — Rtr. Hemavathi S (Project Chair), Rtr. Syed Saqlain Mustaq (International Service Director), and Rtr. Dhikshith R (President). The initiative successfully promoted awareness about Ayurveda while fostering learning, connection, and international fellowship under Team Elev8.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of AIMS, RID 3192
Avenues of Service: Community Service, Professional Development, Club Service

Name: Mind Fiesta
Description:
The Mind Fiesta: How Well Do You Know Rotaract? online quiz, hosted by the Rotaract Club of Swoyambhu, was an engaging and enjoyable initiative designed to strengthen fellowship and test Rotaractors’ knowledge in a fun, interactive way. Conducted exclusively on 7th November 2025, the quiz offered participants a light and refreshing experience while revisiting the core values, history, and spirit of Rotaract.

Rotaractors from various clubs across districts joined the event, making it a vibrant and collaborative celebration. The quiz format was simple and flexible, with no time limit, ensuring that participants could attempt it comfortably while still enjoying a sense of friendly competition.

To qualify as a collaborating club, a minimum of three participants were required to submit their responses, which encouraged collective involvement and teamwork. Members appreciated the easy flow of the quiz and the opportunity it provided to reconnect with the fundamentals of Rotaract learning.

The event successfully promoted fellowship, awareness, and engagement within the Rotaract community. Organized under the leadership of Rtr. Jenisha Gautam (International Service Director, Rotaract Club of Swoyambhu), Mind Fiesta stood out as a simple yet impactful initiative that celebrated knowledge, unity, and the joy of being part of the global Rotaract family.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Swoyambhu

Name: Stress Management
Description:
The Stress Management online session, organized by the Rotaract Club of Delhi Pegasus, RID 3012, was successfully conducted on 10th November 2025 from 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM via Google Meet. The session aimed to help participants understand the roots of stress, recognize its impact, and learn simple yet effective strategies to manage it in daily life.

The resource person for the event, Ms. Dhruvi Shah, a Counselling Psychologist, delivered an insightful and relatable talk on how stress affects both the mind and body. She discussed practical methods such as mindful breathing, grounding exercises, time management, healthy routine building, and the importance of emotional awareness. The session also highlighted how small lifestyle adjustments can significantly help in reducing anxiety and creating a balanced mindset.

Participants actively engaged with questions and reflections, making the session interactive and thought-provoking. Clubs fulfilled the collaboration requirement of a minimum of five members, ensuring strong participation and involvement throughout the session.

The event concluded as an enriching and rejuvenating experience, thanks to the efforts of Rtr. Anushka Kishwar (Project Chair), Rtr. Krishnav Goel (Director, Professional Development), Rtr. Mohd Asif (Secretary), and Rtr. Ayush Raj (President). The session successfully encouraged members to prioritize mental well-being and embrace healthier ways of coping with stress.
Host Club: otaract Club of Delhi Pegasus, RID 3012

Name: Rotaractor’s Odyssey
Description: The Rotaractor’s Odyssey was an enriching online session organized by the Rotaract Club of Bangalore Spandana in collaboration with the Rotaract Clubs of KLE Law College, KSSEM, Bishop Cotton Women’s Christian College, Vijayanagar, Bengaluru HSR, and Jain Evening College on 5th October 2025. The event aimed to help Rotaractors rediscover the deeper purpose of being part of the movement — the “why” behind Rotaract. Featuring Rtr. Syed Aejaz Ahamed, President of the Rotaract Club of Dubai Downtown, as the resource person, the session offered valuable insights into how Rotaract shapes individuals through leadership, service, and meaningful connections. Drawing from his own experiences, Rtr. Syed shared inspiring anecdotes and practical wisdom that highlighted the power of service and fellowship in personal and professional growth. The session’s interactive nature encouraged participants to reflect on their individual Rotaract journeys, understand their roles in creating impact, and find renewed motivation to contribute more meaningfully to their clubs and communities. Beyond learning, the event fostered inter-club bonding and strengthened ties among Rotaractors from different institutions, promoting collaboration and the exchange of ideas across the district. The Rotaractor’s Odyssey concluded on a highly positive note, leaving every attendee inspired to not just be a member of Rotaract, but to truly live its essence — “Fellowship Through Service.” The session successfully rekindled enthusiasm, reinforced purpose, and celebrated the unifying spirit that defines the Rotaract movement.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of Bangalore Spandana

Name: District Sergeants Meeting
Description:
The District Sergeants’ Meeting for Naada Habba 2025 was a dynamic and collaborative gathering that brought together sergeants-at-arms from various Rotaract Clubs across District 3191. The session was convened with the objective of ensuring seamless planning, coordination, and discipline for the upcoming Naada Habba – Karnataka Cultural Celebration, one of the district’s most anticipated flagship events.

The meeting began with a warm welcome and an introduction to the agenda, followed by detailed discussions led by District Sergeant-at-Arms and District Officials. The focus was on event protocols, role assignments, crowd management strategies, and maintaining the decorum and spirit of the celebration. Attendees were guided on effective communication, coordination between clubs, and upholding the values of Rotaract throughout the event.

The session served as an excellent platform for interaction and knowledge-sharing, where sergeants exchanged ideas, addressed potential challenges, and aligned on strategies to ensure smooth execution during Naada Habba.

Representing the Rotaract Club of KSSEM, our club sergeants actively participated in the meeting, contributing valuable insights and demonstrating leadership in planning responsibilities. The gathering strengthened teamwork and instilled a sense of pride and readiness among all attendees.

The District Sergeants’ Meeting set the tone for a well-organized and disciplined Naada Habba, reflecting the unity, culture, and collaborative spirit that define Rotaract District 3191

Name: Learning the Languages of Emotions
Description:
The Rotaract Club of KSSEM cordially invites you to an insightful and transformative webinar titled “Learning the Languages of Emotions.” This session is designed to help individuals understand the depth of their emotions, decode the reasons behind their feelings, and explore how emotional intelligence shapes the way we think, act, and connect with others.

In a world where emotions often take a backseat to logic, this event offers a refreshing opportunity to pause and reflect. Guided by Hemalatha, an accomplished Teacher, Soft Skills Trainer, and Public Speaker, participants will embark on a journey of emotional discovery and self-awareness. The session will delve into understanding emotions, mastering emotional responses, building stronger interpersonal connections, and growing through emotional intelligence.

By the end of this one-hour experience, you will not only gain clarity about your inner emotional world but also learn how to apply emotional intelligence in real-life situations to lead with empathy, resilience, and balance.

Join us on 30th October 2025, from 7:00 PM to 8:00 PM, on Google Meet, for an evening that promises to inspire growth and introspection.

This event is proudly organized under the leadership of Rtr. Lohith J, Community Service Director, and Rtr. Sahana, Professional Development Director. Together, they aim to create a space where learning meets emotion, and knowledge meets heart.

Because understanding emotions is the first step toward understanding ourselves and those around us.

Name: Royal Assembly 1.0
Description:
Setting the strategic direction for an entire Rotaract district requires vision, collaboration, and strong leadership. “Royal Assembly 1.0”, hosted at BMS College of Engineering, embodied all these qualities, serving as a high-level gathering of Rotaract leaders from across District 3191. The assembly brought together club presidents, secretaries, and district officials for an interactive and purposeful session on strategic planning, coordination, and district alignment.

The event commenced with a formal inauguration and insightful addresses by esteemed district officials, who shared their guidance for the upcoming term. Updates on district-wide initiatives, upcoming events, and projects were presented, allowing clubs to synchronize their plans with the district’s overarching goals. One of the most engaging parts of the session was the discussion on proposed amendments to the district bylaws, which promoted open dialogue, transparency, and collective decision-making.

The assembly also celebrated excellence through a recognition ceremony, honoring outstanding clubs and Rotaractors for their contributions. The Rotaract Club of KSSEM stood proud as it received the prestigious Diamond Citation for excellence in club performance. Additionally, two of our exemplary members — Rtr. Bhuvan and Rtr. Tanushree — were honored with the Best Rotaractor Awards, a moment of immense pride for the entire club.

Beyond recognition, the event fostered unity, understanding, and renewed motivation among all attendees. The Royal Assembly 1.0 truly strengthened the district’s collective spirit, leaving every participant inspired, informed, and ready to lead their clubs toward greater impact and service.

Name: Deepavali Donation Drive
Description:
Event Name: Deepavali Donation Drive
Conducted by: Rotaract Club of KSSEM (RID 3191)
Date: 19th October 2025
Venue: Tiruvannamalai, Tamil Nadu
Participants from our club: Rotaract Club of KSSEM

The Rotaract Club of KSSEM took a heartfelt initiative on the occasion of Deepavali by extending support to a family in Tiruvannamalai, Tamil Nadu. The family had reached out requesting assistance for purchasing new clothes and essential ration items to celebrate the festival with joy and dignity.

Understanding their situation, our club took full responsibility and contributed ₹3000 to help them fulfil their festive needs. The members also ensured that the family successfully utilized the amount to purchase the required items, bringing light and happiness into their home during the festive season.

This standalone act of kindness reflected the club’s strong commitment to community welfare and compassion. Through this simple yet meaningful gesture, the *\Rotaract Club of KSSEM aimed to spread the true spirit of Deepavali — the festival of lights, love, and giving.

Name: Naivedyam 4.0
Description:
Event Name: Naivedyam 4.0
Conducted by: Rotaract Club of R.V.C.E. (RID 3190)
Date: 18th October 2025
Venue: Across Bangalore City

The Rotaract Club of KSSEM had the privilege of collaborating in Naivedyam 4.0, the flagship international service initiative organized by the Rotaract Club of R.V.C.E. The initiative aimed to spread the message that even the smallest act of kindness—like sharing a meal—can create a profound impact in society.

Through this noble effort, Rotaractors came together with the shared vision of ensuring that no one sleeps hungry. Our club contributed ₹1000 towards the initiative, joining hands in supporting the mission to provide nourishing meals to the underprivileged across Bangalore city.

The collaboration reflected the true spirit of service and compassion, symbolizing unity beyond club and district boundaries. As part of the partnership, our club also promoted the event through social media platforms to encourage awareness and participation in such impactful causes.

By being a part of Naivedyam 4.0, our club strengthened its commitment to community service and global goodwill, reaffirming the belief that small contributions, when united, can lead to significant change.
Host Club: Rotaract Club of R.V.C.E
Areas of Focus: Providing Clean Water, Sanitation and Hygiene, Promoting Peace

Name: Diwali Celebration with Specially Abled Individuals
Description:
Diwali Celebration with Specially Abled Individuals

The Rotaract Club of KSSEM, in collaboration with its Parent Rotary, celebrated a truly heartwarming and meaningful Diwali with the residents of Siva Sakthi Homes, a residential home for the intellectually challenged. The event took place on Sunday, 19th October 2025, at 4:30 PM onwards, filling the evening with light, laughter, and togetherness that perfectly captured the spirit of Diwali.

This memorable celebration was guided by the thoughtful leadership of Rtn. Rtr. Karthik Sir, whose dedication and efforts ensured that every moment was beautifully organized. The gracious presence of Rtn. Srinivas Sir, President of our Parent Rotary Club, added great warmth and inspiration to the event. Their active participation and words of encouragement made the celebration even more special, strengthening the bond between Rotaract and Rotary.

Our club contributed ₹2000 towards the arrangements, which was used to purchase crackers and diyas. The entire venue glowed with the light of hundreds of diyas, spreading positivity and festive cheer. Members from both the Rotaract Club and the Parent Rotary joined hands to light diyas, burst crackers, share sweets, and spend quality time with the residents of Siva Sakthi Homes. Each smile, each shared moment, and each sparkle of light reflected true joy and compassion.

The collaboration with our Parent Rotary stood as the heart of this celebration, highlighting the shared spirit of unity, empathy, and service. It reminded everyone that Diwali is not just about lighting lamps, but about brightening lives with love and kindness.

This event will be remembered as a day that touched hearts and brought people together, proving that when service meets compassion, every moment becomes a celebration of humanity.
Areas of Focus: Promoting Peace, Saving Mothers and Children
Avenues of Service: Community Service, Club Service, Public Relations

Name: Samskruthi Sangama 2.0
Description:
The Rotaract Club of KSSEM, in collaboration with the Rotaract Club of Mapusa, proudly hosted Samskruthi Sangama 2.0 on 15th October 2025 at 8:30 PM. This virtual cultural exchange program was a delightful celebration of India’s unity in diversity, bringing together Rotaractors from Karnataka and Goa to share, learn, and cherish each other’s rich cultural heritage.

The evening unfolded with vibrant presentations, where the Rotaract Club of KSSEM beautifully showcased the cultural grandeur of Karnataka—its historic architecture, royal dynasties, classical art forms, literature, cuisine, and colorful festivities that reflect the state’s timeless legacy. On the other hand, the Rotaract Club of Mapusa offered a captivating glimpse into the Goan culture, highlighting its spirited traditions, music, festivals, architecture, and unique blend of Indian and Portuguese influences that define its charm.

Conducted in an interactive and engaging online format, the session blended creativity, storytelling, and tradition, offering participants a memorable experience of cultural exchange and fellowship.

Samskruthi Sangama 2.0 successfully built bridges of understanding, strengthened inter-club relations, and celebrated the essence of cultural unity—reminding everyone that beyond languages and borders, it is the shared spirit of respect, joy, and togetherness that truly defines India. 🇮🇳✨

Name: Dandiya
Description:
Date: 11th October 2025
Time: 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM
Venue: KSSEM Badminton Court
Project Chairs: Rtr. Sakthi V Maheswari & Rtr. G. Pavan Kumar
Funds Utilized: ₹16,868
Guest of Honour: Rtn. Srinivas Naidu R., President, Rotary Club of Bangalore Vidyapeeta
DJ: DJ Madhan

The Rotaract Club of KSSEM lit up the campus with vibrant colours, rhythmic beats, and festive energy as it hosted the college’s first-ever Dandiya Celebration on 11th October 2025. The event marked a joyful fusion of culture, unity, and community spirit, bringing together students, faculty, and Rotaractors for an afternoon filled with music, dance, and laughter.

The badminton court was transformed into a dazzling dance floor, adorned with colourful decorations and traditional flair. With DJ Madhan setting the perfect beats, the crowd swayed to the rhythm of Dandiya, celebrating the festival of Navratri in true style. Energizing snacks and refreshments ensured that the enthusiasm never dipped throughout the event.

A team of 14 dedicated volunteers worked tirelessly behind the scenes—handling decorations, logistics, and participant engagement—to ensure flawless execution. The presence of Rtn. Srinivas Naidu R., our esteemed guest and sponsor, added immense value to the occasion. His words of encouragement and generous support elevated the morale of every Rotaractor involved.

For the organizing team, the event was more than just a festivity; it was a dream realized. Witnessing the badminton court transform into a vibrant hub of joy and togetherness symbolized what Rotaract stands for — leadership through celebration and unity through service.

The Dandiya 2025 celebration will forever remain one of KSSEM’s most cherished and spirited moments!

The walk and rally culminated at the MLR Convention Centre, Whitefield, where impactful sessions were held on polio eradication, public health, and the ongoing efforts of Rotary International.

Over 30 enthusiastic members from our club actively joined the METROTHON, showcasing our dedication to service and advocacy. Participants proudly wore exclusive event T-shirts, networked with fellow Rotaractors and Rotarians, and spread the message of hope and health through engaging interactions.

The initiative not only promoted awareness but also inspired young leaders to continue supporting humanitarian causes. The Rotaract Club of KSSEM remains committed to #EndPolioNow and to creating a world free from preventable diseases. 💪🌍

Description:
The Be the Reason online awareness session, organized under Project Snehdhara by the Rotaract Club of Delhi South Central, sponsored by the Rotary Club of Delhi South Central, was successfully conducted on 18th October 2025 at 8:00 PM via Google Meet. Conducted with the belief that “kindness to animals is kindness to ourselves,” the drive encouraged Rotaractors to actively contribute to the welfare of stray animals and birds.

The initiative emphasized that small gestures of compassion, such as providing food or water, can create a ripple effect of positivity and awareness within the community.
